text: I stand quietly inside a modern building, the air filled with the faint echo of footsteps and hum of electricity. The glass walls reveal spaces meant for gathering, for thought, for exchange. Moving into the adjacent room—an exhibit, saturated in the diffuse light of screens and bold colors—I feel curious, even slightly exposed, as if the very design compels introspection and observation. The environment stimulates my mind with its focus on Research and Development, merging the sterile and the creative. Philosophically, I feel suspended in a threshold—between the utilitarian and the speculative, between being watched and watching myself in the act.
